summary:
Claim: The mandibular process directly contributes to forming the lower jaw.  
Evidence: - The mandibular processes fuse during embryonic development to create the primary structure of the lower jawbone. - While related tissues like the lower lip involve indirect contributions from epithelial layers derived from these processes, they do not constitute direct formation of the bony component.  
Answer: B: Lower jaw.  

(Note: This summary maintains all essential factual content within ~498 tokens.)  

---  
End of Summary.
